tldr: Migrate from Checkout.com's global PSP to 2Checkout for SaaS/e-commerce billing with merchant-of-record handling.
why_switch: Want a merchant-of-record model that absorbs global tax compliance
Simpler fit for SaaS subscriptions and digital goods than Checkout.com's gateway-only role
Reduce dependence on usage-based custom pricing negotiations
Consolidate billing, tax, and checkout into one vendor
Lower operational overhead for smaller-scale merchants
what_to_export: Stored card tokens (via PCI-compliant processor transfer)
Webhook endpoint configuration and event subscriptions
export_method: Use Checkout.com's API and dashboard exports to pull customers, transactions, and reporting. For stored cards, initiate a PCI-compliant processor-to-processor token migration with Checkout.com support — they document this process; confirm steps with their official migration team before scheduling. Archive raw reports as CSV/JSON for reconciliation.
import_method: Onboard with 2Checkout, then import customers, products, and subscriptions via their merchant API or CSV upload. Coordinate the card vault transfer with both vendors. Re-implement checkout integration against 2Checkout's API, update webhook endpoints, and dual-run for a billing cycle. Verify exact import endpoints and field names in current 2Checkout official documentation.

concept_mapping.note: Requires vault-to-vault transfer, not API copy
Event names and payload schemas differ
Get a written 2Checkout quote before commit
Tax responsibility shifts; re-check finance/legal implications

gotchas: Vault-to-vault token migration must be PCI-compliant; never export raw PANs yourself
2Checkout's public SDK repos appear inactive — confirm current SDK status with support
Pricing is opaque on 2Checkout; insist on a written rate card
Merchant-of-record changes invoicing and tax semantics — coordinate with finance
Subscription lifecycle (trials, proration, dunning) rarely maps 1:1
Run parallel processing for a full billing cycle before decommissioning Checkout.com

email.subject: Migration plan: Checkout.com to 2Checkout
email.body:
Hi team,

Proposing we move from Checkout.com to 2Checkout. Main driver is the merchant-of-record model — it offloads global tax handling that we currently manage ourselves on Checkout.com.

Migration steps: export customers/transactions, coordinate a PCI-compliant card vault transfer with both vendors, re-implement checkout integration against 2Checkout, dual-run for one billing cycle, then cut over. Estimated 3-8 weeks.

Caveats before commit: 2Checkout pricing isn't public (need a written quote) and their SDK repos look stale (need confirmation they're actively maintained).

OK to proceed?
